meta_title: For Students: Antigravity project template to Solve can't collaborate (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome can't collaborate. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Antigravity project template to Solve Collaboration
content.seo_description: Master team collaboration by using the Antigravity project template. An intermediate guide for students to build scalable AI apps with Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Collaboration bottlenecks destroy momentum. By deploying the Antigravity project template, you establish a standardized environment that enforces strict state management and modular architecture. This eliminates 'it works on my machine' issues, allowing you to focus on shipping features rather than debugging local environment discrepancies with your teammates.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks coding in isolation, your local environment a fragile ecosystem of specific versions and hidden environment variables. When it comes time to invite a collaborator, the friction is immediate: broken dependencies, mismatched configs, and the crushing realization that your code is a silo. This is the silent killer of student portfolios and early-stage SaaS ventures.

But what if your project was inherently collaborative by design? By shifting to the Antigravity project template, you transform your codebase from a private experiment into a production-ready, team-first asset. We are going to bridge the gap between solo development and professional engineering, ensuring your next project is built to scale with others.

steps.title: Initialize the Antigravity Template
steps.description: Establish the foundational directory structure that enforces modularity and standardized pathing.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new project using the Antigravity base template, ensuring strict adherence to the modular file structure for team scalability.

steps.title: Define Environment Schema
steps.description: Create a shared .env.example file that explicitly lists required keys, preventing collaboration friction.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust .env.example file based on the current architecture, including documentation for each required API key.

steps.title: Implement Dependency Injection
steps.description: Decouple services to ensure that team members can swap out modules without breaking the core logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the core logic to use dependency injection, allowing for modular service swapping in a collaborative environment.

steps.title: Configure Collaborative Git Hooks
steps.description: Automate linting and testing on pre-commit to ensure team code quality remains high.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Setup husky pre-commit hooks that run linting and unit tests before any push is allowed.

steps.title: Deploy Shared Preview Environment
steps.description: Set up a CI/CD pipeline that generates a unique URL for every pull request, allowing your team to review changes live.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that deploys a preview environment for every pull request initiated in this project.

pro_tips: Always use absolute imports within the Antigravity template to avoid 'import hell' when moving files between directories.
Treat your README.md as an API contract; if a feature isn't documented there, it doesn't exist for your team.
Implement feature flags immediately, even for solo projects, to practice toggling functionality without merging branches.
Use Claude Code to write your unit tests *before* you write the feature; this acts as the blueprint for your collaborators.
Keep your main branch locked; enforce a 'two-approval' policy for all PRs to maintain high-quality code standards.
resources: Git Best Practices for Teams: https://git-scm.com/book/en/v2
content.next_steps: Once your environment is unified, start by creating a shared 'Contribution.md' file to outline your project's coding standards, then invite your first teammate to clone the repo and verify that the environment boots in under 60 seconds.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting collaboration friction hold your career back. By adopting professional-grade templates, you demonstrate you aren't just a coder, but an engineer who understands systems. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
